top_findings.summary: The skill's primary purpose is to read Google Calendar events and GitHub commit history for meeting preparation and summaries, which only require read access. However, it requests full OAuth scope for Google Calendar (`https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar`, which includes write/modify permissions) and a GitHub Personal Access Token with `repo` scope (which grants full read/write access to repositories). These permissions are excessive for the stated read-only operations, posing a risk of overreach if the skill or an attacker gains unintended write capabilities. More appropriate scopes would be `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly` for Google Calendar and a GitHub token with limited read-only scopes (e.g., `repo:read`).
